recommendation: Add a skill-audit lint that flags any user-invocable:false SKILL.md whose description carries operational detail (file paths, step-by-step, enum/predicate lists) or exceeds the skill-design length bound (~140 chars). The description of a non-triggerable skill is identity + named caller only; operational detail there duplicates the body + README and silently drifts stale. The sdd-trim-internal-descriptions sweep fixed 39 files by hand, but `pnpm verify` passed both before and after — there is no mechanical guard, so the duplication-drift will reaccumulate. Codify the skill-design 'identity + caller' rule as an enforced audit check (and, if warranted, a builder-spec assertion), so this whole failure-mode corpus is prevented mechanically rather than swept periodically.
evidence: sdd-trim-internal-descriptions.plan.md (chore/hygiene, HEAD 1812364d): recurring-pattern signal — CR #121's impl-judge flagged plan-retirement's internal description as STALE (a second copy of the body/README contract kept out of sync); this CR removed the same operational-detail overhang across 39 user-invocable:false skills in plugins/sdd, plugins/aced, plugins/cyberlegion. Fix was manual; the skill audit has no description-content/length check for internal skills (grep of packages/cyberplace audit/skill sources: none), so `pnpm verify` was green with the verbose descriptions in place. Cause: duplication-drift / operating-doc-out-of-sync.
